pattern: The extra edge either creates a node with two parents, a cycle, or both; identifying the conflicting in-edge and a cycle via union-find lets us decide exactly which edge to drop.
hints: An added edge breaks the rooted-tree property in one of two ways: some node gains a second parent, or a cycle forms.
First scan for a node that has two incoming edges and remember those two candidate edges.
Run union-find skipping the second candidate; if a cycle still appears, the answer is the cycle edge (or the first candidate when there is a two-parent node), otherwise it is the second candidate.

edge_inputs.description: node three has two parents, no cycle
cycle present, every node has one parent
two parents and a cycle, must pick the first candidate
two parents where later candidate is removable
star with one extra cross edge forming a cycle

stress.description: build a random rooted tree then add one extra valid directed edge
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randrange(3, max(4, size) + 1)
    nodes = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(nodes)
    root = nodes[0]
    parent = {}
    edges = []
    for idx in range(1, n):
        child = nodes[idx]
        par = nodes[rng.randrange(0, idx)]
        parent[child] = par
        edges.append([par, child])
    # add one extra edge that is not already present and u != v
    existing = set((u, v) for u, v in edges)
    attempts = 0
    while True:
        u = rng.randrange(1, n + 1)
        v = rng.randrange(1, n + 1)
        attempts += 1
        if u != v and (u, v) not in existing:
            edges.append([u, v])
            break
        if attempts > 500:
            edges.append([root, nodes[1]])
            break
    # insert the extra edge at a random position to vary 'last occurrence'
    extra = edges.pop()
    pos = rng.randrange(0, len(edges) + 1)
    edges.insert(pos, extra)
    return [edges]
stress.note: always a rooted tree plus exactly one added edge, matching the problem premise
